observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Accountant reclassify the amount
granted as seed capital fund to the farmer’s associations recorded to expense or
liability accounts to the proper asset account “Other Receivables” (1-03-06-990)
by making the following adjusting entry:

Other Receivables ₱9,550,000.00
Prior Period Adjustment ₱ 100,000.00
Trust Liabilities ₱4,300,000.00
Trust Liabilities – DRRMF ₱2,100,000.00
Due to NGAs ₱3,050,000.00

We further recommended that Management make the necessary
adjustments in the books of accounts for the seed capital assistance granted as a
loan to the beneficiaries of the backyard cattle fattening project in CY 2020 and
prior years when the Municipality started to appropriate funds for the said
project from its own Internal Revenue Allotment.

We furthermore recommended that, henceforth, payments of seed
capital assistance to the members of the Cattle Fattener’s Associations should
be taken up in the books as a receivable through account “Other Receivables”
(1-03-06-990) pursuant to COA Circular No. 2015-009 dated December 1, 2015.

We recommended that Management install internal control mechanisms
by using the RIS to request and issue supplies and materials carried in stock and
to consolidate the same in the SSMI, which shall be submitted to the Municipal
Accountant for recording to the appropriate expense accounts for the supplies
consumed.

Further, we recommended that Management direct the Municipal
Accountant to stop the practice of recording purchases of supplies and materials
directly to expense accounts and henceforth, to record purchases and utilization
of supplies and materials in accordance with Section 114 of the NGAS Manual
for LGUs, Volume I and Section IV of COA Circular No. 2014-002 dated April
15, 2014, to fairly present the expense and inventory accounts in the financial
statements.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant reclassify the cost of
the 17 sets of handheld radios recorded to expense account “Other Supplies and
Materials Expense” (5-02-03-990) to the proper asset account “Communication
Equipment” (1-07-05-070) by making the following adjusting entry:

Communication Equipment ₱33,150.00
Prior Period Adjustment ₱33,150.00
(The account Prior Period Adjustment was recommended because the books
of accounts as of December 31, 2021 were already closed.)

We also recommended that, henceforth, Management refer to the
description of accounts as guide in recording expenses in the books of accounts
pursuant to COA Circular No. 2015-009 dated December 1, 2015 in order to
avoid erroneous entries.

We recommended that Management undertake their procurements
through Competitive Bidding in accordance with Section 10, Rule IV of the IRR
of R.A. No. 9184, except as provided in Rule XVI of the IRR.

We also recommended that Management observe the judicious
application of Shopping as the alternative mode of procurement when the
conditions provided under Section 52.1 of the Revised IRR of R.A. No. 9184 are
established.

We recommended that before any repairs and maintenance of motor
vehicles and heavy equipment are undertaken, Management require the
personnel in charge to prepare the following reports, namely: a) pre-repair
evaluation report and approved detailed plans showing in sufficient detail the
scope of work/extent of repair to be done, b) report of waste materials, c)
document indicating the history of repair, and d) post-inspection report.

We further recommended that the Municipal Accountant ensure that
disbursements for the repairs and maintenance of motor vehicles and heavy
equipment are supported with the required documents enumerated under
Section 9.1.3.4 of COA Circular No. 2012-001.

We recommended that Management stop the payment of additional
honoraria to the Barangay Health Workers (BHWs) and Barangay Nutrition
Scholars (BNS) pursuant to DBM Local Budget Circular No. 63, Section 95 of
R.A. No. 7160 and Article IX-B of the 1987 Philippine Constitution to avoid
incurring illegal expenditures.

We recommended that Management submit the aforementioned lacking
documents as required under Section 13.1 of COA Circular 2012-001 to enable
us to properly evaluate the propriety of the disbursements.

We further recommended that the Municipal Treasurer submit a
certification that there are no arrearages in Real Property Taxes on the acquired
properties as of the date of purchase.

We furthermore recommended that, henceforth, the Municipal
Accountant ensure the completeness of the supporting documents for every
transaction of the Municipality before payment is made in compliance with
Section 4.6 of PD No. 1445.

We recommended that Management realign the unutilized continuing
appropriations for the cattle fattening project and SEA-K program for priority
development projects signified in the DBM, DOF, and DILG JMC No. 1 dated
November 4, 2020, and henceforth, comply with the limitations on the use of
the 20% DF as outlined in the said Circular.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant reclassify the cost of
one unit chainsaw recorded to account “Other Machinery and Equipment” (1-
07-05-990) to the proper account “Disaster Response and Rescue Equipment”
(1-07-05-090) by making the following adjusting entry:

Disaster Response and Rescue Equipment ₱49,000.00
Other Machinery and Equipment ₱49,000.00

We further recommended that, henceforth, Management refer to the
description of accounts as a guide in recording expenses in the books of accounts
pursuant to COA Circular No. 2015-009 dated December 1, 2015, to avoid
erroneous entries.

We recommended that the MDRRMC and MDRRMO review and update
annually the MDRRMFIP to include prior years’ unexpended LDRRMF in
accordance with Sections 5.1.2 and 5.1.15 of COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated
September 12, 2012, in order to enhance disaster/calamity preparedness and
response in view of the availability of more funds.

We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer transfer the depository
account maintained with the RBBI to either Land Bank of the Philippines,
Dumaguete Branch or Development Bank of the Philippines, Dumaguete
Branch, in compliance with Section 311 of RA No. 7160 and DOF Department
Circular No. 01-2017 dated May 11, 2017.

We recommended that Management ensure systematic procedures while
strictly adhering to existing guidelines set forth under DSWD Memorandum
Circular No. 09 series of 2020 in the implementation of SAP.

We recommended that Management submit to the Auditor copy of the
Mayor’s/Business Permit, original OSS of the suppliers, duly notarized, using
the form prescribed under RA No. 11469 and RA No. 11494, and PE
certification stating that it exerted all efforts to secure the most advantageous
price to the government. We further recommended that the Municipal
Accountant ensure that the documentary requirements are satisfactorily
complied with in accordance with GPPB Resolution No. 18-2020 dated
September 16, 2020 and GPPB Circular No. 01-2020 dated April 6, 2020 and
that the DVs are duly supported with complete documents pursuant to Section
4(6) of PD No. 1445.

We recommended that Management require the personnel concerned or the end-user duly authorized by the Head of the Procuring Entity to completely
and properly fill up the relevant and required information in the PO pursuant to
Item B of COA Circular No. 96-010 dated August 15, 1996.

We recommended that the BAC require the suppliers to submit the
original notarized OSS before or after award of contract but before payment is
made and include a warranty clause in the contract or POs for procurements
through emergency purchase in accordance withh GPPB Circular No. 01-2020
dated April 6, 2020. We further recommended that the Municipal Accountant
ensure the completeness of the documentary requirements for emergency
procurement before payment is made.

We recommended that Management:
a. Submit the following:
i. An explanation for proceeding with the canvass on the basis of a
purchase request which lacked details and specifications and with
bids which were unusually too similar, and despite the lack of details;
ii. An explanation as to why the caterers who participated in the canvass
were able to come up with their bids despite the lack of a menu;
iii. A breakdown of each caterer’s menu offering; and
iv. A corrected Acceptance and Inspection Report specifying the items
inspected.
b. Ensure that the procedures for Shopping under Section 52.1(a) of the
2016 RIRR of RA No. 9184 are satisfactorily adhered to.

We recommended that Management require the Bids and Awards
Committee to submit justification for the procurement of the items above the
highest suggested retail price of the DOH. We further recommended that
Management procure medical supplies based on the SRP set by appropriate
government agencies to ensure that the Municipality get the most advantageous
prices.

We recommended that the following documents be immediately
submitted to the Auditor to determine compliance with the conditions on the
grant of COVID-19 Hazard Pay set forth in Paragraph 4 of DBM Budget
Circular No. 2020-01 dated March 24, 2020, to wit:
a. Authority for the entitlement of the allowance; and
b. IATF Resolution of the Risk Certification in the workplace
Likewise, we recommended that Management submit copy of the
approved contracts of personnel under COS and JO scheme.

We recommended that:
a. Management establish a procedure in identifying PHWs who shall be
exposed to high risk or low risk hazard including the duration of such
exposure duly approved by the Secretary of Health to support claims for
hazard pay; and
b. The Municipal Accountant require all the PHWs to support their claims
for hazard pay with proof of actual exposure to specific health hazards
and occupational risks with certification from appropriate agencies and
other documents required under Section 5.8 of COA Circular No. 2012-
001 dated June 14, 2012.

We recommended that Management adhere strictly with the rates of
hazard pay per month applied to the monthly basic salary in accordance with
Section 3.3 of DBM-DOH Joint Circular No. 1 series of 2016 dated July 15,
2016 subject to the Personal Services limitation in LGU budget, to ensure that
health workers are justly compensated.
We also recommended that Management submit an explanation why the
nine PHWs were granted their hazard pay for the period October to December
2020 only.

We recommended that Management prepare a one-time report to the
National Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Council (NDRRMC),
through the Office of the Civil Defense (OCD) on all donations received by the
Municipality and furnish the Auditor a copy of the (a) Acknowledgement
Receipts of the Donations In-Kind; (b) Proof of receipt by and distribution to the
beneficiaries; and (c) Inventory of remaining undistributed items, if any, in
accordance with the provisions of COA Circular No. 2020-009 dated April 21,
2020.

We recommended that Management secure valid official receipts from
New Bian Yek Commercial, Inc. to properly substantiate payments of 1,700 bags
of 50 kilograms rice worth ₱3,332,500.00.

We also recommended that Management submit the following documents
or a written explanation why the same could not be submitted:
a) Relief distribution sheets for the relief and welfare goods showing the
names of the beneficiaries with their signatures, kind and quantity of
items received by each beneficiary, and the date of receipt;
b) List of frontliners and persons under monitoring who were catered
with meals during ECQ and GCQ, duly signed by them; and
c) List of passengers who availed of the free transport service during the
implementation of ECQ, duly signed by them.

Lastly, we recommended that Management ensure that disbursements
are duly substantiated with the required documents pursuant to Section 4(6) of
PD No. 1445.

We recommended that the Municipality insure the projects of the General Service Fund (GSIS) in order that the government may be indemnified from any damage to or loss of the project in case of fire, earthquake, storm or other calamity.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Treasurer inquired with the GSIS regarding the insurance cover for projects funded under the BUB. The GSIS stated it does not cover insurance for such projects; insurance for such projects was not available.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipality procure common-use supplies from PS-DBM and, henceforth, comply with the provisions of Administrative Order No. 17 dated July 28, 2011 and DBM Circular Letter No. 2011-6 dated August 25, 2011.
observations.managementAction: Management deemed it impractical due to distance and time constraints to procure from PS-DBM.
observations.reason: Management deemed it impractical due to distance and time constraints.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management prepare the following reversal
entry:

Account Title Account Code Debit Credit
Land 1-07-01-010 ₱11,677,081.19
Government Equity 3-01-01-010 ₱11,677,081.19

We also recommended that Management exhaust all means to determine
the acquisition costs of Lot Nos. 1840, 1841, 1851-B, 1852, 1855, 1856, 1894, and
3181 so that appropriate adjustment in the recorded costs may be made
accordingly.

Lastly, we recommended that, henceforth, Management adhere to the
provisions set forth under PPSAS No. 17 for the accounting treatment for
property, plant, and equipment to present fairly the financial statements.

We recommended that Management:

a) Submit the loan agreement together with the certified true copy of the SB
Resolution bearing the signatures of all concurring SB members, BSP
Opinion of the Monetary Board together with the requisite documents,
BLGF-DOF certifications, certification by the Municipal Budget Officer,
Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer noted by the LCE, the
pertinent Promissory Note and other collateral documents, and the
supplemental AIP;
b) Renegotiate on a fixed interest rate, with primordial consideration of the
Municipality’s capability to repay the loan within the incumbency of the
officials’ 3-year term in office; and
c) Report the loan proceeds and the corresponding capital outlay in the SCBAA
for CY 2019.

We recommended that, henceforth, Management furnish the Auditor
with a copy of contracts together with the supporting documents within the
reglementary period of five working days from perfection, in accordance with
Section 3.1.1 of COA Circular No. 2009-001 dated February 12, 2009.

We recommended that Management submit the aforementioned lacking
documents as required under Section 13.1 of COA Circular 2012-001 to enable
us to properly evaluate the propriety of the claim. Further, we recommended
that Municipal Treasurer submit a certification that there were no arrearages in
Real Property Taxes on the acquired property as of the date of purchase.

We also recommended that, henceforth, the Municipal Accountant
ensure the completeness of the supporting documents for every transaction of
the Municipality before payment is made in compliance with Section 4(6) of PD
No. 1445.

We recommended that Management effect the increase of the fidelity
bond of the Disbursing Officer Designate commensurate to his accountability, in
accordance with the Bureau of Treasury Circular No. 02-2009 dated August 6,
2009, in order to protect the interest of the Municipal government.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor appoint or designate
additional DO to avoid the granting of multiple cash advances to the incumbent
DO.

We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer require the issuance of
ORs (AF No. 51) from the VMLP, Inc. and PCL, Inc. - Negros Oriental
Federation for the payments made for membership dues/fees in CY 2019. We
further recommended that the Municipality withhold future payments until the
leagues comply with this requirement.

We recommended and Management agreed that, henceforth, the Local
Chief Executive direct all department heads concerned to submit their
respective duly accomplished AAPSI and assign a personnel to consolidate the
same for submission to the Auditor within 60 days upon receipt of the AAR for
the succeeding years.

We recommended and Management agreed to submit the GAD Plan
and Budget to the PPDO and DILG Provincial Office within the prescribed
period in accordance with Section 4.0 of PCW-DBM-DILG-NEDA JMC No.
2016-01 dated February 12, 2016.
We recommended that Management, henceforth, ensure that projects under the 20% DF conform to DILG-DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2017-1 dated February 22, 2017.
observations.managementAction: In CY 2019, most of the projects included under the 20% Development Fund conformed to the list of projects authorized under DILG-DBM JMC No. 2017-1 dated February 22, 2017. However, a total appropriations of ₱1,000,000.00 was intended for MOOE of solid waste management and clearing/declogging of drainage canals.
observations.reason: No reason given by Management.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer enforce collection of delinquent real property taxes by conducting intensive tax campaign in every barangay and by sending Notices of Delinquencies to all delinquent taxpayers. We further recommended that the Municipality avail of the remedies in collecting delinquent real property taxes provided in Sections 256 to 260 of RA No. 7160.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality conducted massive tax campaign during barangay outreach. Notices to top 50 delinquent taxpayers were already sent.
observations.reason: No reason given by Management.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that: a) The Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer reconcile the difference between the RPT and SET Receivable account balances together with the Municipal Assessor, henceforth, conduct periodic reconciliation, and maintain subsidiary ledgers for accounts RPT Receivable and SET Receivable; b) The Municipal Assessor prepare and submit to the Municipal Treasurer an assessment roll containing a list of all persons whose real properties have been newly assessed or reassessed and the values of such properties on or before the thirty-first (31st) day of December each year; c) The Municipal Treasurer maintain Real Property Tax Account Register/Taxpayer’s index card and furnish the Municipal Accountant a duly certified list of taxpayers with the amount due and collectible for the current year as basis for recording the RPT Receivable and SET Receivable accounts in compliance with Section 20 of the NGAS Manual for LGUs, Volume I; and d) The Municipal Accountant maintains subsidiary ledgers for accounts RPT Receivable and SET Receivable in accordance with Section 10 of the NGAS Manual for LGUs, Volume II.
observations.managementAction: Management was already in the process of reconciling and verifying the account balances.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management determine if the services rendered by the laborers under the job order status are necessary in the operations of the Municipality, otherwise, immediately terminate their services. If the services are necessary and essential, Management should ensure that payrolls covering payment of services were properly supported with complete documentary requirements as required under COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012. We also recommended that Management immediately submit to the Office of the Auditor the pertinent work contract/job order and duly verified/accepted accomplishment report of each laborer who worked in the implementation of various projects charged against the LDRRMF and 20% DF, respectively, in CY 2018. We further recommended that Management immediately submit to the Auditor all common and additional documentary requirements for the purchase of lots enumerated under Section 13.1 of COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Assessor had started preparing the requisite documents needed for the processing of the land titles. The Municipal Accountant reviewed and made sure that payrolls covering payment of services were properly supported as required. The Municipal Accountant also required submission of pertinent documents.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that, henceforth, the PPAs to be included in the annual LDRRM Plan should conform to the PPAs enumerated in NDRRMC, DBM and DILG JMC No. 2013-1 dated March 25, 2013 and DILG MC No. 2012-73 dated April 17, 2012.
observations.managementAction: Management prioritized the projects and the LDRRM Plan conformed to the PPAs and DILG MC No. 2012-73 dated April 17, 2012.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that, henceforth, maximize the use of the 70% LDRRMF by implementing projects that will eliminate flood risks and lessen adverse impacts of hazards and calamities. We further recommended that the Management train and equip volunteers as well as residents to be able to respond to any disaster or calamity.
observations.managementAction: Not Relevant documents needed for validation were not submitted. In CY 2019, of ₱6,650,000.00 appropriations for the LDRRMF, only ₱17,500.00 or 0.26% was utilized during the year. On the other hand, of ₱12,307,164.26 continuing appropriations, only ₱2,648,700.00 or 21.52% was utilized during the year.
observations.reason: Not Relevant documents needed for validation were not submitted.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant reclassify the prior years’ post-planting maintenance expenses capitalized to account Plants and Trees to the proper accounts by making the following adjusting entry: Maintenance and Other Operating Expenses 74,640.00; Prior Period Adjustment 297,420.00; Plant and Trees 372,060.00. We further recommended that henceforth, stop from recording the expenses related to post-planting maintenance to account Plants and Trees and record these under Maintenance and Other Operating Expenses.
observations.managementAction: Adjusting entry was made per JEV# 100-19-04-1126B dated April 5, 2019 amounting to ₱217,716.23. Also, JEV No. 100-18-11-2746-B dated November 30, 2018 amounting to ₱42,800.00 was prepared to take up unrecorded Lots 1840, 1841, 1851-B, 1852, 1855, 1856, 1894, and 3181.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that thereafter, the Municipality properly identify the GAD issues and concerns and use the GAD fund to implement programs/projects/activities that are aligned with PCW, DILG, DBM, and NEDA Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-01 dated July 18, 2013.
observations.managementAction: Management included PhilHealth insurance coverage of indigent families, and purchase of medicines for medical missions. Management did not give any reason for failing to implement the recommendation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management direct the Municipal Planning and Development Officer to establish the GAD database storage of necessary information in aid of effective gender-responsive planning, programming and policy formulation in accordance with PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA JMC No. 2013-1 to ensure that the PPAs are aligned with GAD-related undertakings so that the intended beneficiaries are assured of the services, protection, and other assistance that may be provided.
observations.managementAction: Still in the process of formulating the GAD Database. Conduct of survey for CBMS was implemented. Survey was conducted by BHW for family profiling.
observations.reason: Already appropriated a certain amount for survey for CBMS.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management undertake a concerted effort to fast track the implementation of projects outlined under the 20% DF so that the constituents can enjoy the benefits that can be derived from the projects.
observations.managementAction: The CY 2019 Registry of Allotment and Obligations of the 20% DF with total appropriations of ₱30,538,291.49 showed that out of 52 projects intended to be implemented during the year, only 16 projects were started during the year with a total cost of ₱9,327,948.62 as of December 31, 2019.
observations.reason: No reason given by Management.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LCPC prepare the Annual Work and Financial Plan and allocate more funds for PPAs that would promote children’s rights and welfare and protect them against abuse, neglect, exploitation and violence as envisioned under RA No. 9344. We also recommended that the Council prepare the Local Development and Investment Plans for Children, and Local State of Children’s Report as required under DILG Memorandum Circular No. 2012-120 dated July 4, 2012.
observations.managementAction: The CY 2019 Registry of Allotment and Obligations showed that the amount of ₱528,000.00 was appropriated for the payment of honoraria of Day Care Workers, which was not eligible for funding from the allocation for the LCPC.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local School Board (LSB) refrain from charging expenses not listed under Section 272 of RA No. 7160 and its related DepEd, DBM and DILG Joint Circular, and review the PPAs included in the SEF budget to ensure proper utilization thereof, and for the Municipal Accountant to secure from the DepEd Accountant a certification that request on the use of SEF is net of the budgetary provision for the same or related item(s) in the budget of the DepEd and the ECCD Council.
observations.managementAction: Not Relevant documents needed for validation were not submitted.
observations.reason: Not Relevant documents needed for validation were not submitted.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipality establish and operate a sanitary landfill in accordance with Sections 40 and 41 of RA No. 9003 and meantime, take action to convert the existing open dump site into a controlled dump and hasten its closure within a specific time frame. We further recommended that the municipal ordinance on solid waste management be strictly enforced and all barangays be involved in the implementation of waste reduction and segregation at source in accordance with RA No. 9003.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality failed to establish and operate a sanitary landfill due to limited financial resources. Only residual wastes were collected and deposited in the dumpsite.
observations.reason: Due to limited financial resources.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LDRRM Officer include in its LDRRMFIP the PPAs to be funded by the unexpended LDRRMF. We further recommended that the LDRRM Officer submit the Monthly Report on the Sources and Utilization of DRRM on or before the 15th day after the end of each month to the Office of the Auditor pursuant to Section 5.1.5 of COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012.
observations.managementAction: Monthly Report on the Sources and Utilization of DRRM were submitted but not within the reglementary period. Monthly Utilization Reports submitted were only for January to June 2019.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management prioritize the filing of application for titling of the recorded properties to hasten the issuance of certificates of title in favor of the Municipality. We further recommended the Municipal Assessor maintain property cards and the Municipal Treasurer maintain property ledger cards and reconcile their records with the Office of the Municipal Assessor.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Assessor initially started the filing of application for the titling of the recorded properties in CY 2017. Also, the Office failed to maintain property cards.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the OIC-Municipal Accountant draw a journal entry voucher to record all lots owned by the Municipality. We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Inventory Committee to conduct a physical count thereof and reconcile the same with related records to be maintained by the Offices of the Municipal Treasurer, Municipal Accountant and Municipal Assessor in accordance with existing regulations.
observations.managementAction: JEV No. 100-17-12-2817 dated December 29, 2017 amounting to ₱217,716.23 was prepared. Also, JEV No. 100-18-11-2746-B dated November 30, 2018 amounting to ₱42,800.00 was prepared to take up unrecorded Lots 1840, 1841, 1851-B, 1852, 1855, 1856, 1894, and 3181. Inventory Committee was created and no physical count was conducted.
observations.reason: No Inventory Committee was created and no physical count was conducted.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that, henceforth, the members of the BAC/TWG allow only eligible prospective bidders to participate in the public bidding for the procurement of goods and services. We further recommended that the BAC/TWG should conduct post-qualification in accordance with Section 34, Rule X of the 2016 Revised Implementing Rules and Regulations of RA No. 9184.
observations.managementAction: Some POs were submitted belatedly and PO procedures were improved. Several procurements were done through shopping.
observations.reason: Some POs, like POs for purchases of supplies and medicines lack some of the basic information.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that, henceforth, the Municipality conduct competitive bidding in the procurement of capital cattle in accordance with Section 10 of the Revised IRR of RA No. 9184 to ensure that the most advantageous price is obtained and more farmer beneficiaries are benefited. We also recommended that the more farmer beneficiaries be required to return the grant of capital assistance if not utilized as intended.
observations.managementAction: According to the Municipal Agriculturist, the grant of assistance was returned as each farmer beneficiary utilized the amount of ₱20,000.00 released to them.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the personnel preparing the purchase orders prepare the PO and indicate all the basic information thereon as required under COA Circular No. 96-010 dated August 15, 1996 to ensure delivery of items that meet the specification within the required timeframe and the collection of liquidated damages in case of late delivery.
observations.managementAction: Some POs particularly those for purchases of medicines already contained the required basic information. Some procurements were done through shopping.
observations.reason: Some POs lacked the basic information.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipality close its deposit accounts with private banks and deposit the same to a government financial institution granting higher interest rates.
observations.managementAction: Management opted to maintain their depository account in RBB for convenience and security purposes and to help the local economy of the Municipality.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipality fill up the positions of Municipal Treasurer, Municipal Accountant, Municipal Health Officer and Local Civil Registrar pursuant to Sections 470, 474, 478 and 479 of RA No. 7160. We also recommended that should there be designations, designees should hold the position for one year only and be designated to positions within the level they are currently occupying.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Treasurer, Municipal Accountant, Municipal Health Officer, and Local Civil Registrar were already appointed or assigned as permanent in CY 2017, CY 2018, and March 2020, respectively.
observations.reason: For the position of Municipal Health Officer, personnel presently working at the Accounting Office were qualified and appointed.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant record purchases of supplies and materials to appropriate inventory accounts instead of recognizing expenses in order to properly account for the receipts and issuances of supplies and materials in accordance with Section 114 of the NGAS Manual for LGUs Volume II.
observations.managementAction: Purchases of supplies and medicines were taken up in the books as inventories before recognizing expenses for its subsequent issuance.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Bids and Awards Committee strictly observe Section 52.1 in the procurement of goods.
observations.managementAction: Some procurements were done through Shopping. Management did not give any reason.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the OIC-Municipal Accountant see to it that 10% warranty security is deducted from the amount claimed to protect the interest of the LGU pursuant to Section 62.1 of the Revised Implementing Rules and Regulations of RA No. 9184.
observations.managementAction: Some procurements of medicines were partially compliant and adjustments were being enforced.
observations.reason: Relevant documents needed for validation were not submitted.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the OIC-Municipal Treasurer require the taxpayers to submit an Income Statement/Statement of Receipts and Expenditures when applying for renewal of business taxes to ascertain the accuracy of the declared gross receipts subject to tax. If the examination of the books could not be conducted immediately, the taxpayers may be required to submit their preceding year’s financial statements as temporary bases in computing the business taxes.
observations.managementAction: Only big business establishments were examined. Small business establishments were interviewed and required to fill-out forms to show their Income/Receipts and Expenditures.
observations.reason: Lack of manpower.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipality revise the Solid Waste Management Plan following the Annotated Outlined prescribed by the National Solid Waste Commission and submit the same to the National Solid Waste Commission for approval pursuant to Section 16 of RA No. 9003 for an effective solid waste collection and disposal.
observations.managementAction: A ten-year SWMP was submitted. Initial schedule for final coaching was deferred due to COVID-19.
observations.reason: Final coaching was deferred due to COVID-19.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management: a. Conduct a Physical Inventory of all involved properties to determine the existence and physical condition of these assets; b. Reclassify the fully depreciated and unserviceable assets from the Property and Plant and Equipment account to Other Asset account and to look into the disbursement vouchers and other documents pertaining to the unbooked assets and record the same in the books of accounts; c. Locate the missing hand held radio issued to the former barangay captain of Barangay Calangag and the Iwata Time Recorder; and d. Refrain from storing government properties in a private residence/place and have the Municipal Treasurer locate and retrieve missing items. Also, the Municipal Treasurer to locate the engine previously installed in the LGU owned patrol boat and get back the 70CC, Econo, engine kept in a private residence.
observations.managementAction: Inventory Committee was created and no physical count was conducted since CY 2017. The Honda motorcycle was under repair at a repair shop while the Iwata time recorder was stored in the HR Office. The patrol boat engine was retrieved and a new body was fabricated.
observations.reason: No available personnel could be assigned to identify the fully depreciated and unserviceable assets; no physical count was conducted.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management: a. Immediately send demand letters to the concerned accountable officer/other agency officials/employees; b. If the employees still fail to settle their cash advances after due notice, instruct the municipal treasurer to withhold the payment of their salaries; c. Henceforth, require in particular the Municipal Accountant and other concerned officials/employees to strictly observe the provisions of COA Circular No. 97-002 in the grant, utilization and liquidation of cash advances.
observations.managementAction: Closely monitored the liquidation of cash advances. Demand letters were sent to concerned persons; some retired, transferred or deceased and the receivables could not be collected.
observations.reason: Some accountable officers with unliquidated cash advances were no longer in the service due to retirement, resignation, transfer, and death.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management: a. Conduct a physical inventory of all involved properties; b. Settle the Due to NGAS account by submitting Liquidation Reports to the grantors so that the latter’s books are also adjusted accordingly, before effecting the transfer to the General Fund; c. Based on the completed physical inventory, require that properties, plant and equipment under the trust fund be immediately transferred to the specific account of general fund for proper presentation of the financial statements pursuant to Section 104 of NGAS; d. Require the LGU to maintain and update regularly a properly accomplished Property, Plant and Equipment Ledger Cards (PPELC) for easy verification and reference both by the auditee and auditor; e. Prepare an adjusting journal entry relative to the infiltration machine; Other Mach. and Eqipt. P12,585,505.00; Other Payables P1,377,975.00; Artesian Wells, Reservoir, Pumping Stn.& Conduits P12,585,505.00; Guarantee Dep. Payable P1,377,975.00.
observations.managementAction: Physical inventory of completed projects was conducted in CY 2019. Completed projects were not yet transferred. PPELC were not updated due to lack of manpower.
observations.reason: PPELC were not updated due to lack of manpower; completed projects were not yet transferred.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that: a. Members of the Bids and Awards Committee/Technical Working Group should conduct thorough evaluation and review eligibility requirements for the Procurement of Goods and Infrastructure Project; b. The BAC allow only eligible prospective bidders to participate in the public bidding for the procurement of goods and services; c. In the post qualification, the BAC/TWG should verify, validate, and ascertain the genuineness, validity and accuracy of the legal, technical and financial documents submitted by the bidder with the LCB before awarding the same; d. Personnel preparing the purchase orders should strictly indicate the basic data on the PO pursuant to Section B.1 of COA Circular 96-010 dated August 15, 1996; and e. The agency should submit the lacking documents pursuant to Section 138 (f) of the Government Accounting and Auditing Manual, Volume 1.
observations.managementAction: Some validation documents were submitted belatedly. Evaluation procedures were improved.
observations.reason: Relevant documents needed for validation were not submitted earlier.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer intensify collection of real property taxes due from different taxpayers by issuing notices of demand for payment of delinquencies, and apply the remedies provided under Section 254 and 258 of the Local Government Code, especially against those with delinquent accounts of more than two (2) years if they fail to heed the demand.
observations.managementAction: The Office of the Treasurer was updating the list with collection letters and collection efforts were intensified. Some collection letters were sent to delinquent taxpayers.
observations.reason: Not all delinquent real property taxpayers were sent collection letters.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant prepare and submit the monthly trial balances and financial statements within the reglementary period in accordance with Section 70 of the Manual on NGAS for LGUs, otherwise, we shall be constrained to withhold the salary of concerned officials and implement other sanctions and penalties as provided for under our laws.
observations.managementAction: Monthly trial balances and financial statements with the related supporting schedules were submitted but there were delays due to lack of manpower.
observations.reason: Delayed transmittal of bank statements from the depository banks; lack of manpower.

observations.recommendation: Required the Municipal Accountant to assign a specific personnel to prepare and submit the monthly bank reconciliation statements to COA and the depository bank so that timely verification and review can be made and discrepancies immediately identified and corrected.
observations.managementAction: Bank Reconciliation Statements were submitted to COA for the months of January to June 2019 only.
observations.reason: Delayed transmittal of Bank Statements from the depository banks.

observations.recommendation: We recommended increasing rental rates or implement measures to reduce operating costs of the municipality’s economic enterprises, and require the municipal treasurer to accelerate the collection of unpaid rentals by sending bills to delinquent stall or space lessees, invoking the sanctions provided under the Contract of Lease to make the collection more effective. In addition, look for additional revenue sources, such as charging minimal fees for the use of the market’s comfort rooms. Install government plates to the three municipal vehicles which bear private plates (Mitsubishi Pajero and two Mitsubishi Canter trucks with plates numbers mentioned) and mark all municipal vehicles with the words “FOR OFFICIAL USE ONLY” for security reasons.
observations.managementAction: All other vehicles were marked “FOR OFFICIAL USE ONLY” except for the Mitsubishi Pajero which still bears private plate and not marked with the words “FOR OFFICIAL USE ONLY”. Operational measures to increase collection were implemented.
observations.reason: The Mitsubishi Pajero still bears private plate.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant mark all municipal vehicles with the words “FOR OFFICIAL USE ONLY” with the name of the municipality underneath, and instruct the treasurer and accountant to desist from paying expenses for the maintenance of these vehicles unless the requirements under COA Circular No. 75-6 are complied with.
observations.managementAction: GSV732, GRS608 and GRS701 plates were marked and most municipal vehicles were marked with “FOR OFFICIAL USE ONLY” with the name of the municipality underneath. The treasurer and accountant complied with COA Circular No. 75-6 for most vehicles.
observations.reason: One vehicle (Mitsubishi Pajero) still not marked as required.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management undertake a concerted effort to fast track the implementation of projects outlined and that the constituents can enjoy the benefits that can be derived from the projects.
observations.reason: Management opted to utilize first the Bottom-Up Budgeting (BuB) Assistance to Depressed Municipalities (ADM) funds received since the funds will be reverted back to the National Treasury if remained unutilized at year end.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LCPC prepare the Annual Work and Financial Plan and allocate more funds for PPAs that would promote children’s right and welfare and protect them against abuse, neglect, exploitation and violence as envisioned under RA No. 9344. We further recommended that the Council prepare the Local Development and Investment Plans for Children, and Local State of Children’s Report as required under DILG Memorandum Circular No. 2012-120.
observations.reason: LCPC was still utilized for payment of honoraria of Day Care Workers. Local Development and Investment Plans for Children, and Local State of Children’s Report as required under DILG Memorandum Circular No. 2012-120 were not prepared.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LSB refrain from charging expenses not listed under Section 272 of RA No. 7160 and its related DepEd, DBM and DILG Joint Circular, and review the PPAs when included in the SEF budget to ensure proper utilization thereof, and request the DepEd Accountant to secure certification on the use of SEF or the budgetary provision for the same or related item(s) in the budget of the DepEd.
observations.reason: Most of the expenses which request were not properly included in the SEF were already incurred and paid.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management prioritize the establishment and operation of a sanitary landfill in accordance with Sections 40 and 41 of RA No. 9003 and meantime, take action to convert the existing open dump site into a controlled dump and hasten its closure within a specific time frame. We further recommended that the municipal ordinance on solid waste management be strictly enforced and all barangays be involved in the implementation of waste reduction and segregation at source in accordance with RA No. 9003.
observations.reason: Disposal of solid waste in an open dumpsite was still practiced in the Municipality due to limited financial resources.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management immediately stop the use of GAD funds for the projects not related to GAD and, henceforth, include in their Annual GAD Plan and Budget programs and activities which would address gender issues only.
observations.reason: The GAD Fund was still utilized for projects not related to GAD because Management did not adhere to the Auditor’s recommendations.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management refrain from utilizing the LDRRMF for purposes not related to disaster preparedness, prevention and mitigation, and henceforth, adhere strictly to the provisions of RA No. 10121 and NDRRMC, DBM and DILG Joint Memorandum Circulars in the utilization of the 5% LDRRMF.
observations.reason: Management thought that the projects were properly chargeable to the Fund.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the MDRRM Council include in its LDRRMFIP the PPAs to be funded by the unexpended LDRRMF. We further recommended that the LDRRM Officer submit the Monthly Report on the Sources and Utilization of DRRM on or before the 15th day after the end of each month to the Office of the Auditor pursuant to Section 5.1.5 of COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012.
observations.reason: The PPAs to be funded from the LDRRMF were still not included in the LDRRMFIP. Monthly Report in the Sources and Utilization of DRRM were not submitted within the reglementary period.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that, henceforth, the members of the BAC/TWG allow only eligible prospective bidders to participate in the public bidding for the procurement of goods and services. We further recommended that the BAC/TWG should conduct post-qualification in accordance with Section 34, Rule X of the 2016 Revised Implementing Rules and Regulations of RA No. 9184.

We recommended that the Municipality procure common-use supplies from PS-DBM and, henceforth, comply with the provisions of Administrative Order No. 17 dated July 28, 2011 and DBM Circular Letter No. 2011-6 dated August 25, 2011.
observations.reason: Management did not give any reason.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Bids and Awards Committee strictly observe Section 52.1 of the Revised Implementing Rules and Regulations of RA No. 9184 in the procurement of goods.
observations.reason: Several procurements were done through shopping.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the OIC-Municipal Treasurer ensure that 10% warranty security is deducted from the amount claimed to protect the interest of the LGU pursuant to 62.1 of the Revised Implementing Rules and Regulations of RA No. 9184.
observations.reason: Some procurements of medicines were not deducted with 10% warranty security as required.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that: a. Members of the Bids and Awards Committee/Technical Working Group conduct thorough evaluation and review of the eligibility requirements for the Procurement of Goods and Infrastructure Project. b. The BAC allow only eligible prospective bidders to participate in the public bidding for the procurement of goods and services. c. In the post-qualification, the BAC/TWG should verify, validate, and ascertain the genuineness, validity and accuracy of the legal, technical and financial documents submitted by the bidder with the LCB before awarding the same. d. Personnel preparing the purchase orders should strictly indicate the basic data on the PO pursuant to Section B.1 of COA Circular 96-010 dated August 15, 1996. e. The agency should submit the lacking documents pursuant to Section 138 (f) of the Government Accounting and Auditing Manual, Volume 1.

We recommended that Management, henceforth, ensure that projects
under the 20% DF conform to DILG-DBM JMC No. 2017-1 dated February 22,
2017 so that the desired socio-economic development and environmental
management outcomes as envisioned by the national government may be
achieved.

We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer enforce collection of
delinquent real property taxes by conducting intensive tax campaign in every
barangay and by sending Notices of Delinquencies to all delinquent taxpayers.
We further recommended that the Municipality avail of the remedies in
collecting delinquent real property taxes provided in Sections 256 to 260 of RA
No. 7160.

We recommended that:
a) The Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer reconcile the difference
between the RPT and SET Receivables account balances totaling
₱18,359,226.24 and henceforth, conduct periodic reconciliation, together with
the Municipal Assessor, of the total collectibles from the Basic and Special
Education Tax with the balances of the RPT Receivables and SET
Receivables accounts to avoid accumulation of unreconciled differences;
b) The Municipal Assessor prepare and submit to the Municipal Treasurer an
assessment roll containing a list of all persons whose real properties have
been newly assessed or reassessed and the values of such properties on or
before the thirty-first (31st) day of December each year;
c) The Municipal Treasurer maintain Real Property Tax Account
Register/Taxpayer’s index card and furnish the Municipal Accountant a duly
certified list of taxpayers with the amount due and collectible for the current
year as basis for recording the RPT Receivable and SET Receivable accounts
in compliance with Section 20 of the NGAS Manual for LGUs, Volume I; and
d) The Municipal Accountant maintain subsidiary ledgers for accounts RPT
Receivable and SET Receivable in accordance with Section 10 of the NGAS
Manual for LGUs, Volume II.

We recommended that Management determine if the services rendered
by the laborers under the job order status are necessary in the operations of the
Municipality, otherwise, Management should immediately terminate their
services. If the services are necessary and essential, we recommended that the
Municipal Accountant review and make sure that payrolls covering payment of
services are properly supported with complete documentary requirements as
required under COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012.

We also recommended that Management immediately submit to the
Office of the Auditor the pertinent work contract/job order and duly
verified/accepted accomplishment report of each laborer who worked in the
implementation of various projects charged against the LDRRMF and 20% DF,
respectively, in CY 2018.

We further recommended and Management agreed to submit
immediately to the Auditor all common and additional documentary
requirements for the purchase of lots enumerated under Section 13.1 of COA
Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant immediately transfer the prior years’ unexpended balance of the Quick Response Fund and the DRRMF-MOOE to the Special Trust Fund under the account “Trust Liability-DRRM” in the Trust Fund books.
observations.managementAction: Unspent DRRM Funds from CYs 2015 and 2016 were transferred to the Trust Fund.
observations.reason: JEV No. 100-17-12-2817 dated December 29, 2017 amounting to ₱217,716.23 was prepared.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management prioritize the filing of application for titling of the recorded properties to hasten the issuance of certificates of title in favor of the Municipality. We further recommended the Municipal Assessor maintain property cards and the Municipal Treasurer maintain property cards and reconcile with other offices.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Assessor started the filing of application for titling the recorded properties in CY 2017. Also, the Office failed to maintain property cards.
observations.reason: The Municipal Assessor initially recorded the titling of the properties in CY 2017. Implementing personnel constraints affected full compliance.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the OIC-Municipal Accountant draw a journal entry voucher to record all lots owned by the Municipality. We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Inventory Committee to conduct the physical count thereof and reconcile the same with related records maintained by the Offices of the Municipal Treasurer, Municipal Accountant and Municipal Assessor in accordance with existing regulations.
observations.managementAction: JEV No. 100-17-12-2817 dated December 29, 2017 amounting to ₱217,716.23 was prepared.
observations.reason: The physical count was not conducted and the Inventory Committee was not created.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipality insure the project with the General Insurance Fund (GSIS) in order that the government may be indemnified from any damage to or loss of the project in case of fire, earthquake, storm or other calamity.
observations.managementAction: Inquired with the GSIS regarding the insurance coverage.
observations.reason: According to the Municipal Treasurer, GSIS does not cover insurance for the projects funded under the BUB.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that, henceforth, the Municipality conduct competitive bidding in the procurement of capital cattle in accordance with Section 10 of the Revised IRR of RA No. 9184 to ensure that the most advantageous price is obtained and more farmer beneficiaries are benefited. We also recommended that the more farmer beneficiaries be required to return the grant of capital assistance.
observations.reason: According to the Municipal Agriculturist, no excess grant of capital assistance was returned as each farmer beneficiary utilized the amount of ₱20,000.00 released to them.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that personnel preparing the PO indicate all the basic information thereon as required under COA Circular No. 96-010 dated August 15, 1996 to ensure delivery of items that meet the specification within the required timeframe and the collection of liquidated damages in case of late delivery.
observations.reason: Some POs, particularly those for catering services for supplies and medicines already contained the basic information. Some POs lacked some of the basic information.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipality close its deposit accounts with the PVB and RBB and deposit the same to a government financial institution granting high interest rates.
observations.reason: The Municipality opted to maintain their depository account in RBB because it is more convenient on their part.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor fill up the positions of Municipal Treasurer, Municipal Accountant, Municipal Health Officer and Local Civil Registrar pursuant to the Sections 470, 474, 478 and 479 of RA No. 7160. We also recommended that should there be designations, designees should hold the position for one year only and be designated to positions within the level they are currently occupying.
observations.managementAction: A Municipal Health Officer was employed as permanent in CY 2017. Positions of Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer were submitted to the BLGF for approval.
observations.reason: For the positions of Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer, no personnel presently working at each respective office is qualified.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant record purchases of supplies and materials to the books as appropriate inventory accounts instead of expenses in order to properly account for the receipts and issuances of supplies and materials in accordance with Section 114 of the NGAS Manual for LGUs Volume II.
observations.reason: Purchases of supplies and medicines were still taken up in the books as outright expenses instead of inventory.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the OIC-Municipal Treasurer require the taxpayers to submit an Income Business Statement/Statement of Receipts and Expenditures when applying for renewal of business taxes to ascertain the accuracy of the declared gross receipts subject to tax. If the examination of the books could not be conducted immediately, require the taxpayers to submit their preceding year’s financial statements as temporary bases in computing the business taxes.
observations.managementAction: Only big business establishments were examined. Small establishments were only interviewed and required to fill-out forms to show their Income/Receipts and Expenditures.
observations.reason: Not all business taxpayers were examined due to lack of manpower.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management revise the Solid Waste Management Plan following the Annotated Outlined prescribed by the National Solid Waste Commission and submit the same to the National Solid Waste Commission for approval pursuant to Section 16 of RA No. 9003 for an effective solid waste collection and disposal.
observations.managementAction: A New Tax Ordinance was implemented.
observations.reason: The Municipal Management did not revise their SWMP because they only had one year left in the original 10-year Plan.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management: a. The Mayor to strictly require the Inventory Committee to conduct the physical count of properties and reconcile the same with related property and accounting records and for the municipal treasurer’s office to maintain property cards in accordance with existing regulations. b. The Accountant to reclassify the fully depreciated and unserviceable assets from the Property and Plant and Equipment account to Other Asset account and to look into the disbursement vouchers and other documents pertaining to the unbooked assets and record the same in the books of accounts. c. The Municipal Treasurer to locate the missing hand held radio issued to the former barangay captain of Barangay Calangag and the Iwata Time Recorder amounting to ₱24,642.50 and ₱14,950.00, respectively. Also, locate the engine previously installed in the LGU owned patrol boat and get back the 70CC, Econo, power kept in a private residence and henceforth, refrain from storing government properties in a private residence/place.
observations.managementAction: The Honda motorcycle is under repair in a repair shop while the Iwata time recorder is already stored in the HR Office. The engine of the patrol boat was already retrieved and a new body was fabricated. The Honda Bantay Dagat is now being used by the Bantay Dagat. PPELC were not updated due to lack of manpower.
observations.reason: No Inventory Committee was created and no physical count was conducted in CY 2017.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management: a. Immediately send demand letters to the concerned accountable officials/employees. b. If the employees concerned still fail to settle their cash advances after due notice, instruct the municipal treasurer to withhold the payment of their salaries. c. Henceforth, require in particular the Municipal Accountant and other concerned officials/employees to strictly observe the provisions of COA Circular No. 97-002 in the grant, utilization and liquidation of cash advances.
observations.reason: Accountable officers with unliquidated cash advances were no longer in the service due to retirement, resignation, transfer, and death.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management: a. Conduct a physical inventory of all involved properties to determine the existence and physical condition of these assets. b. Settle the Due to NGAs account by submitting Liquidation Reports to the grantors so that the latter’s books are also adjusted accordingly, before effecting the transfer to the General Fund. c. Based on the completed physical inventory, require that properties, plant and equipment under the trust fund be immediately transferred to the specific account of general fund for proper presentation of the financial statements pursuant to Section 104 of NGAS. d. Require the LGU to maintain and regularly update properly accomplished Property, Plant and Equipment Ledger Cards (PPELC) for easy verification and reference both by the auditee and auditor.
observations.managementAction: Completed projects were not yet transferred.
observations.reason: No physical inventory of all involved properties was conducted in CY 2018. PPELC were not updated due to lack of manpower.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer intensify collection of real property taxes due from different delinquent taxpayers by issuing notices of demand for payment of delinquencies, and apply the remedies provided under Section 254 and 258 of the Local Government Code, especially against those with delinquent accounts of more than two (2) years if they fail to heed the demand.
observations.managementAction: The Office of the Treasurer was updating the list with delinquent taxpayers. Collection letters had been sent out to some delinquent taxpayers.
observations.reason: Not all delinquent real property taxes were collected.

observations.recommendation: Require the Municipal Accountant to assign a specific personnel to prepare and submit the monthly bank reconciliation statements and financial statements within the reglementary period in accordance with Section 70 of the Manual on NGAS for LGUs, otherwise, we shall be constrained to withhold the salary of concerned officials and implement other sanctions and penalties as provided under our laws.
observations.reason: Monthly trial balances and financial statements with the related supporting schedules were not submitted on time to COA due to lack of manpower.

observations.recommendation: Require the Municipal Accountant to assign a specific personnel to prepare and submit the monthly bank reconciliation statements to COA and the depository bank so that timely verification and review can be made and discrepancies immediately identified and corrected.
observations.managementAction: Bank Reconciliation Statements were submitted to COA once a year only.
observations.reason: Delayed transmittal of Bank Statements from the depository banks.

observations.recommendation: Consider increasing rental rates or implement measures to reduce operating costs of the municipality’s economic enterprises, and require the municipal treasurer to accelerate the collection of unpaid rentals by sending bills of collection to delinquent stall or space lessees, invoking the sanctions provided under the Contract of Lease to make the collection more effective. In addition, look for additional revenue sources, such as charging minimal fees for the use of the market’s comfort rooms.
observations.managementAction: A New Tax Ordinance was implemented.
observations.reason: Some repairs and improvements on the economic enterprises were not yet implemented.

observations.recommendation: Install government plates to the three municipal vehicles which bear private plates (Mitsubishi Pajero with plates GSV732, two Mitsubishi Canter trucks with plates GRS608 and GRS701) mark all municipal vehicles with the words “FOR OFFICIAL USE ONLY” with the name of the municipality underneath, and instruct the treasurer and accountant to desist from paying expenses for the maintenance of these vehicles unless the requirements under COA Circular No. 75-6 are complied.
observations.managementAction: The two Mitsubishi Canter trucks are marked with the words “FOR OFFICIAL USE ONLY”. The Mitsubishi Pajero still bears private plate and not marked with the words “FOR OFFICIAL USE ONLY” for security reasons.
observations.reason: Delayed implementation.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that management undertake a concerted effort to fast
track the implementation of projects outlined under the 20% DF so that the
constituents can enjoy the benefits that can be derived from the projects.

We recommended that the LCPC prepare the Annual Work and Financial
Plan and allocate more funds for PPAs that would promote children’s right and
welfare and protect them against abuse, neglect, exploitation and violence as
envisioned under R.A. 9344. We further recommended that the Council prepare the
Local Development and Investment Plans for Children, and Local State of
Children’s Report as required under DILG Memorandum Circular No. 2012-120
dated July 4, 2012.

We recommended that the LSB refrain from charging expenses not listed
under Section 272 of R.A. No. 7160 and its related DepEd, DBM and DILG Joint
Circular, review the PPAs included in the SEF budget to ensure proper utilization
thereof, and for the Municipal Accountant to secure from the DepEd Accountant a
certification that request on the use of SEF is net of the budgetary provision for the
same or related item(s) in the budget of the DepEd and the ECCD Council.

We recommended that management prioritize the establishment and
operation of a sanitary landfill in accordance with Sections 40 and 41 of R.A. 9003
and meantime, take action to convert the existing open dump site into a controlled
dump and hasten its closure within a specific time frame. We further recommended
that the municipal ordinance on solid waste management be strictly enforced and all
barangays be involved in the implementation of waste reduction and segregation at
source in accordance with RA No. 9003.
We recommended that the Bids and Awards Committee strictly observed Section 52.1 in the procurement of goods.
observations.managementAction: See AAR
observations.reason: Several procurements were done through shopping.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the OIC-Municipal Treasurer ensure that 10% warranty security is deducted to protect the interest of the LGU pursuant to 62.1 of the Revised Implementing Rules and Regulations of RA 9184.
observations.managementAction: See AAR
observations.reason: The OIC-Municipal Accountant is new and is not aware of the previous year’s recommendation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that: a. Members of the Bids and Awards Committee/Technical Working Group should conduct thorough evaluation and review of the eligibility requirements for the Procurement of Goods and Infrastructure Project. b. The BAC allow only eligible prospective bidders to participate in the public bidding for the procurement of goods and services. c. In the post-qualification, the BAC/TWG should verify, validate, and ascertain the genuineness, validity and accuracy of the legal, technical and financial documents submitted by the bidder with the LCB before awarding the same. d. Personnel preparing the purchase orders should strictly indicate the basic data on the PO pursuant to Section B.1 of COA Circular 96-010 dated August 15, 1996. e. The agency should submit the lacking documents pursuant to Section 138 (f) of the Government Accounting and Auditing Manual, Volume 1.
observations.managementAction: AAR
observations.reason: No reason given.

observations.recommendation: Install government plates to the three municipal vehicles which bear private plates (Mitsubishi Pajero and two Mitsubishi Canter trucks with plates GSV732, GRS608 and GRS701); mark all municipal vehicles with the words “FOR OFFICIAL USE ONLY” with the name of the municipality underneath, and instruct the treasurer and accountant to desist from paying expenses for the maintenance of these vehicles unless the requirements under COA Circular No. 75-6 are complied.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality is still waiting for the release of the red plates from LTO.
observations.reason: The Municipality is waiting for the release of the red plates from LTO.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management prioritize the filing of application for titling of the recorded properties to hasten the issuance of certificates of title in favor of the Municipality. We further recommended the Municipal Treasurer maintain property cards and the Municipal Accountant to update their records and reconcile with the Office of the Municipal Assessor.

We recommended that the OIC-Municipal Accountant draw a journal entry voucher to record all lots owned by the Municipality. We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Inventory Committee to conduct a physical count thereof and reconcile the same with related records maintained by the Offices of the Municipal Treasurer, Municipal Accountant and Municipal Assessor in accordance with existing regulations.

We recommended that the Municipality insure the project with the General Insurance Fund of the General Service Insurance System (GSIS) in order that the government may be indemnified from any damage to or loss of the project in case of fire, earthquake, storm or other calamity.

We recommended and management agreed that, henceforth, the members of the BAC/TWG allow only eligible prospective bidders to participate in the public bidding for the procurement of goods and services. We further recommended that the BAC/TWG should conduct post-qualification in accordance with Section 34, Rule X of the 2016 Revised Implementing Rules and Regulations of RA 9184.

We recommended that, henceforth, the Municipality conduct competitive bidding in the procurement of cattle in accordance with Section 10 of the Revised IRR of RA 9184 to ensure that the most advantageous price is obtained and more farmer beneficiaries are benefited. We also recommend that the farmer beneficiaries be required to return the excess grant of capital assistance.

We recommended and management agreed that personnel assigned to prepare the PO indicate all the basic information thereon as required under COA Circular No. 96-010 dated August 15, 1996 to ensure delivery of items that meet the specification within the required timeframe and the collection of liquidated damages in case of late delivery.

We recommended and management agreed to fully utilize the one percent of IRA pursuant to DILG Memorandum Circular No. 2012-120 dated July 4, 2012. We also recommended that management give more attention to other PPAs that would truly safeguard and protect the welfare of children.

We recommended that, henceforth, the OIC-Municipal Accountant withhold 10% retention money from the payment of labor in accordance with the provision in the pakyaw agreement to protect the interest of the Municipality in cases of defects or poor workmanship by pakyaw contractors.
We recommended that the Municipality close its deposit accounts with the PVB and RBB and deposit the same to a government financial institution granting high interest rates.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor fill up the positions of Municipal Treasurer, Municipal Accountant, Municipal Health Officer and Local Civil Registrar pursuant to Sections 470, 474, 478 and 479 of RA 7160. We also recommended that should there be designations, designees should hold the position for one year only and be designated to positions within the level they are currently occupying.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant record purchases of supplies and materials to the appropriate inventory accounts instead of expenses in order to properly account for the receipts and issuances of supplies and materials in accordance with Section 114 of the NGAS Manual for LGUs, Volume II.

We recommended that the Local Transition Team conduct an inventory as soon as possible, prepare and submit the report of physical count as of June 30, 2016 to the incumbent Municipal Mayor pursuant to Item B.1 of the Department of the Interior and Local Government Memorandum Circular No. 2016-21 dated February 17, 2016.

We recommended and the OIC-Municipal Accountant agreed to require the DO to submit the documentary requirements enumerated under Sections 1.1 and 1.2.4.1 of COA Circular No. 2012-01 and Section 4.3.3 of COA Circular No. 97-002 dated June 14, 2012 and February 10, 1997.

We recommended and management agreed that the Municipality procure common-use supplies from PS-DBM and, henceforth, comply with the provisions of Administrative Order No. 17 dated July 28, 2011 and DBM Circular Letter No. 2011-6 dated August 25, 2011.

We recommended that the Municipal Social and Development Officer attach the required supporting documents needed in the granting of Capital Assistance for the Livelihood Program (SLP) in order to establish the validity of the transactions made.
observations.managementAction: Documents were AAR already submitted to COA.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the OIC-Municipal Accountant ensure that all claims are duly supported with all the documentary requirements before payment in accordance with Section 4(6) of PD 1445 and Sections 5.7.1 and 9.1 of COA Circular No. 2012-001.
observations.managementAction: AAR already submitted to COA.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the OIC-Municipal Treasurer require the taxpayers to submit an Income Statement/Statement of Receipts and Expenditures when applying for renewal of business taxes to ascertain the accuracy of the declared gross receipts subject to tax. If the examination of the books could not be conducted immediately, the taxpayers may be required to submit their preceding year’s financial statements as temporary bases in computing the business taxes.
observations.managementAction: AAR see reference
observations.reason: Not all business taxpayers were examined due to lack of manpower.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management immediately revise the Solid Waste Management Plan (SWMP) following the Annotated Outlined prescribed by the National Solid Waste Management Commission and submit the same to the National Solid Waste Commission for approval pursuant to Section 16 of RA 9003.
observations.managementAction: AAR Implemented
observations.reason: Completed projects were not updated due to lack of personnel.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the municipal employee responsible for the preparation of the GAD Plan and Budget see to it that policies and programs outlined in the Plan must be appropriately observed.
observations.managementAction: AAR
observations.reason: The four recipients were only enrolled in one semester.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management: a. The Mayor to strictly require the Inventory Committee to conduct the physical count of properties and reconcile the same with related property and accounting records and for the municipal treasurer’s office to maintain property cards in accordance with existing regulations. b. The Accountant to reclassify the fully depreciated and unserviceable assets from the Property Plant and Equipment account to Other Asset account and to look into the disbursement vouchers and other documents pertaining to the unbooked assets and record the same in the book of accounts. c. The Municipal Treasurer to locate the missing hand held radio issued to the former barangay captain of Barangay Calangag and Iwata Time Recorder amounting to ₱24,642.50 and ₱14,950.00 respectively. Also, locate the engine previously installed in the LGU owned patrol boat and get back the 70CC, Econo, motorcycle kept in a private residence and henceforth, refrain from storing government properties in a private residence/place.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2014 Implemented
observations.reason: An Inventory Committee was not created.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Health Officer certify that the drug products being requisitioned or procured fall within and conform with PNDF as required under Executive Order No. 49 and attach the approved written request with corresponding justification for the procured medicines and drugs worth P99,448.10 which were found to be not included in the essential drug list.
observations.managementAction: Drugs and medicines procured in CY 2015 were already supported with the required certification.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Mayor adopt measures to mitigate the effects of the current open dumpsite by fencing the perimeter area and assigning personnel to segregate biodegradable and non-biodegradable wastes for possible recycling. He should request the member of the Sangguniang Bayan for appropriations so that the Solid Waste Management Program can be implemented.
observations.managementAction: The open dumpsite in Bgy. Combado was already closed.

observations.recommendation: We recommend management: a. Immediately send demand letters to the concerned accountable officer/other agency officials/employees. b. If the employees concerned still fail to settle their cash advances after due notice, instruct the municipal treasurer to withhold the payment of their salaries. c. Henceforth, require in particular the Municipal Accountant and other concerned officials/employees to strictly observe the provisions of COA Circular No. 97-002 in the grant, utilization and liquidation of cash advances.
observations.managementAction: Closely monitored
observations.reason: Accountable officers with unliquidated cash advances are those who are no longer in the service concerned due to retirement, resignation, transfer, and death.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management: a. Conduct a Physical Inventory of all involved properties to determine the existence and physical condition of these assets. b. Settle the Due to NGAS account by submitting Liquidation Reports to the grantors so that the latter’s books are also adjusted accordingly, before effecting the transfer to the General Fund. c. Based on the physical inventory, require that properties, plant and equipment under the trust fund be immediately transferred to the specific account of general fund for proper presentation of the financial statements pursuant to Section 104 of NGAS. d. Require the LGU to maintain and regularly update a properly accomplished Property, Plant and Equipment Ledger Cards (PPELC) for easy verification and reference both by the auditee and auditor. e. Prepare an adjusting journal entry relative to the infiltration machine as follows: Other Mach. and Eqipt. P12,585,505.00 Other Payables 1,377,975.00 Artesian Wells, Reservoir, Pumping Stn. & Conduits 12,585,505.00 Guarantee Dep. Payable 1,377,975.00
observations.managementAction: Reclassification of fully depreciated and unserviceable assets has already started. Adjustment made under JEV# 300-13-12667 dated 12/29/13.
observations.reason: Completed projects were not yet transferred and PPELC were not updated due to lack of personnel.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer intensify collection of real property taxes due from different delinquent taxpayers by issuing notices of demand for payment of property tax delinquencies, and to henceforth, apply the remedies provided under Section 254 and 258 of the Local Government Code, especially against those with delinquent accounts of more than two (2) years if they fail to heed the demand.
observations.managementAction: The Office of the Treasurer is updating the list of delinquent taxpayers.
observations.reason: Notices of Delinquency were not yet prepared because the Provincial Government has not yet finalized the real property code.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Mayor adopt measures to ensure the timely submission of monthly trial balances and financial statements and related supporting schedules in accordance with Section 70 of the Manual on NGAS for LGUs, otherwise, we shall be constrained to withhold the salary of concerned officials and implement other sanctions and penalties as provided for under our laws.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2010 Implemented
observations.reason: Monthly trial balances were not submitted on time because of lack of manpower.

observations.recommendation: Require the Municipal Accountant to assign a specific personnel to prepare and submit the monthly bank reconciliation statements to COA and the depository bank so that timely verification and review can be made and discrepancies immediately identified and corrected.
observations.managementAction: Bank Reconciliation Statements were submitted to COA once a year only.
observations.reason: Delayed transmittal of Bank Statements from the depository banks.

observations.recommendation: Consider increasing rental rates or implement measures to reduce operating costs of the municipality’s economic enterprises, and require the municipal treasurer to accelerate the collection of unpaid rentals by sending bills of collection to delinquent stall or space lessees, invoking the sanctions provided under the Contract of Lease to make the collection more effective. In addition, look for additional revenue sources, such as charging minimal fees for the use of the market’s comfort rooms.
observations.managementAction: A New Tax Ordinance was passed for the Municipality.
observations.reason: New Tax Ordinance was implemented; Municipality is waiting for the completion of the new market building.
